commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `sessions_delegate` and `sessions_delegate_batch` tools, registers them in the coding/session tool surface, adds display/catalog metadata, and wires delegate output capture through subagent spawn and cleanup state.

PR surface: Source +644, Tests +460, Other +20. Total +1124 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: the PR adds new child-session delegation tool ids to default profiles/groups while current policy, tests, and docs only treat `sessions_spawn` as the delegation control point.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs human maintainer approval for the new core delegation surface and contributor proof; automation should not own the product/security policy choice.

target.title: feat(agents): add sessions_delegate and sessions_delegate_batch tools
target.body:
## Summary
- Problem: OpenClaw agents can spawn background sessions (`sessions_spawn`) but cannot synchronously delegate a task and receive structured output back as a tool result. Complex parallelizable work runs sequentially or requires manual user coordination.

- Why it matters: Multi-agent orchestration (fan-out/fan-in, map-reduce, pipeline patterns) is blocked without a synchronous delegation primitive.

- What changed:

  - Added `sessions_delegate` tool — blocking spawn-and-wait that returns child output as a tool result

  - Added `sessions_delegate_batch` tool — parallel fan-out of multiple delegate tasks with configurable failure mode

  - Wired up the existing but unused `prepareSubagentSpawn` context engine hook in `subagent-spawn.ts`

  - Registered both tools in tool catalog, display config, and openclaw-tools

- What did NOT change (scope boundary): `sessions_spawn` behavior is untouched. No changes to auto-announce, session lanes, or existing lifecycle hooks. No plugin-layer orchestration patterns yet (map-reduce, pipeline, race) — this PR provides the runtime primitives only.



## Change Type

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra


## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Two new agent tools available: `sessions_delegate` and `sessions_delegate_batch`
- `sessions_delegate`: spawns a child agent, blocks until completion, returns output directly as tool result (default timeout 120s, default cleanup "delete")
- `sessions_delegate_batch`: fans out 1–10 tasks in parallel, returns collected results with per-task status and summary. `failureMode: "partial"` (default) returns completed results even if some fail; `"all"` fails the batch if any task fails.


## Diagram (if applicable)

```text

**Before (sessions_spawn):**

[parent calls sessions_spawn] -> [child runs async] -> [auto-announce as user message] -> [parent resumes on next turn]

**After (sessions_delegate):**

[parent calls sessions_delegate] -> [child runs] -> [tool blocks via agent.wait] -> [reads frozenResultText] -> [returns as tool result in same turn]

**After (sessions_delegate_batch):**

[parent calls sessions_delegate_batch] -> [N children spawn] -> [Promise.allSettled] -> [collect results] -> [return aggregated tool result]

```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `Yes` — two new agent tools, but they reuse existing `spawnSubagentDirect` which enforces depth limits, active children limits, sandbox policies, and visibility guards.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No` — reuses existing `agent.wait` gateway RPC
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes` — new tools registered in catalog
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- Risk + mitigation: New tools inherit all existing spawn guards (max depth, max active children, sandbox mode, session lane isolation). No new escalation paths.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`
target.author: daevmithran
